caseStudy.title: Real-Time Credit Risk Scoring
caseStudy.description: Millisecond-latency credit decisions at loan application scale.
caseStudy.overview: A digital lending platform needed to make real-time credit decisions within the loan application flow, requiring sub-100ms model inference latency at thousands of requests per second while maintaining the accuracy of models trained on rich historical features.
caseStudy.problem: The existing credit scoring process took 45 seconds, requiring applicants to wait for a decision. This friction caused significant drop-off during the application flow. The scoring models used features that were expensive to compute in real time, making direct productionisation of research models infeasible.
caseStudy.solution: A two-tier scoring architecture was designed: a fast real-time score using pre-computed features served from a low-latency store for the immediate decision, followed by an asynchronous enhanced score using richer features for downstream risk management. The feature store ensured consistency between training and serving environments.

results.summary: Applicants now receive instant credit decisions, eliminating the wait-time drop-off that had been a major conversion drag. The two-tier architecture delivers real-time performance without compromising model sophistication.
